summary: Pi recovers from a MID-STREAM bridge tool failure: first call fails, model retries the same command, stream continues, turn completes with the build id
review_criteria: proper formatting — clean segmentation, no run-on/garbled/merged text, no stray control chars or terminal escape codes
human-readable — reads like an assistant working, coherent and natural
correct text <-> tool interleaving order (narration then the tool it describes)
tool calls are the real intended tools (not leaked internal/shell noise where MCP tools were expected)
the final answer is coherent and matches the work performed
real work actually happened (e.g. the file was written to disk)

output.answer: The build ID printed is BUILD_ID_c54ab239c2f7.
output.clean_stream: I will now execute the shell command to read the build ID file.I will retry the execution of the shell command due to the transient failure.The build ID printed is BUILD_ID_c54ab239c2f7.

issues: Missing space between two adjacent delta-streamed sentences: "...specified file.The build ID is..." — a residual minor cosmetic gap in captureRealBridge's delta-vs-block joining (two consecutive delta runs with no block boundary between them get concatenated with nothing, not even a space). Not a regression of the real bug found here.
agent_review.notes: Real bug fixed and verified: this same test previously showed the build id SPLIT MID-STRING ("BUILD_ID_4aa\n94683070c") because captureRealBridge inserted a newline after every content chunk unconditionally, including mid-token deltas. Now delta-aware (mirrors buildMessageModes's existing correct logic) and the build id streams intact. The tool recovered correctly (2 calls, retry succeeded) and the answer is correct.
